title: rosa × dumetorum subsp. platyphylla
intro: Rosa × dumetorum subsp. platyphylla, also known as the wild rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum subsp. platyphylla include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
